steps.title: Locate el Castillo in the table
steps.content:
The passage states el Castillo was built 1,100 to 1,400 years ago. Find the row in the table matching that age range to read off the correct height. The rationale confirms the four heights in the table are 33 m (Great Pyramid, Mexico), 40 m (el Castillo, Belize), 47 m (Pyramid of Sahure, Egypt), and 60 m (Pyramid of Djoser, Egypt).
  • Choice A — '33 meters tall.': 33 m is the height of the Great Pyramid in Mexico, not el Castillo. ✗
  • Choice B — '47 meters tall.': 47 m is the height of the Pyramid of Sahure in Egypt, not el Castillo. ✗
  • Choice C — '40 meters tall.': 40 m matches the el Castillo row in the table. ✓
  • Choice D — '60 meters tall.': 60 m is the height of the Pyramid of Djoser in Egypt, not el Castillo. ✗

steps.title: Confirm the table value
steps.content: El Castillo (Belize, 1,100–1,400 years old) has a table height of 40 meters; all other values belong to different pyramids. That matches choice C — C is correct.
